title: Tal Chhapar Sanctuary
tagline: Tal Chhapar Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Rajasthan

overview.short: Tal Chhapar Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Churu district of northwestern Rajasthan, in the Shekhawati region of India.
overview.description: Tal Chhapar Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Churu district of northwestern Rajasthan, in the Shekhawati region of India. It is known for blackbucks and is also home to a variety of birds. It is situated on the fringe of the Great Indian Desert and between Ratangarh and Sujangarh on the Nokha-Sujangarh state highway.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tal Chhapar Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tal Chhapar Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tal Chhapar Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bikaner Airport (Nal) (124 km) and the nearest railway station is Bikaner Junction (113 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tal Chhapar Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tal Chhapar Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tal Chhapar Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Tal Chhapar Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
